commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining decision is backend support ownership, not a narrow code repair automation can settle.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain blocker was found; the new dependencies and adapted parser remain merge-risk items for maintainer acceptance. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds native Telegram for macOS Postbox import, optional SQLCipher/PyPI dependency setup, doctor/metadata/docs updates, an upstream license notice, and sanitized parser/routing tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection shows current main lacks Postbox support, and the PR discussion provides exact-head real import output for the new path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining decision is backend support ownership, not a narrow code repair automation can settle.

target.title: [codex] import Telegram macOS Postbox archives
target.body:
# Human written summary

The intent of this change is, as written by a human:
> Telegram is stupid and has two different versions on MacOS for reasons. Presumably the original version of Telecrawl was written against the other version of Telegram, whichever that one is, which has a different backing database type, it seems. (App Store vs installer, and they have totally different codebases). Anyway, this ensures telecrawl can import from both database types, because I had the other one installed.

# Changes

- Adds an offline native Telegram for macOS Postbox importer for `.tempkeyEncrypted` plus `account-*/postbox/db/db_sqlite`.
- Keeps one import path: `telecrawl import` auto-detects Telegram Desktop `tdata` first, then the native macOS group container; explicit `--path` values are classified automatically.
- Keeps one dependency setup path: `telecrawl deps install` installs bridge dependencies for both supported local Telegram backends.
- Reports Postbox key/database counts in `doctor` and advertises the `telegram-macos-postbox` local-only scope in crawlkit metadata.
- Adds public sanitized Postbox format fixtures under `internal/telegramdesktop/testdata/postbox` plus parser/routing tests, so review does not depend only on private Telegram rows.

# Tests

- `python3 -B internal/telegramdesktop/scripts/import_postbox.py --self-test --fixture-dir internal/telegramdesktop/testdata/postbox` passed.
- `python3 -B -m py_compile internal/telegramdesktop/scripts/import_postbox.py internal/telegramdesktop/scripts/import_tdata.py` passed.
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c go test -count=1 ./...` passed.
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c go test -count=1 -race ./...` passed.
- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 nixpkgs#golangci-lint -c golangci-lint run` passed.
- `staticcheck ./...` passed under Nix Go.
- Source-built import from a copied Keepcoder dataset passed: `48,980` messages, `358` chats, `9,919` media-tagged messages, oldest `2015-01-25T11:29:57Z`, newest `2026-05-28T16:31:11Z`.
- Raw decrypted Postbox `t7` key sanity check matched the import count: `48,980` unique local messages.

# Risks

- Postbox parsing is local-cache best effort. It imports text, peer names where locally decodable, timestamps, direction, sender IDs, and media tag categories, but not full Telegram cloud metadata parity with the Telethon `tdata` path.
- SQLCipher availability matters for Postbox imports; Docker and `deps install` include the needed Python/native dependencies.

# Follow-ups

- Extend Postbox media cache extraction beyond media tag categories.
- Add a fuller public SQLCipher/Postbox corpus if a non-private sample can be produced.
target.author: joshp123

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: fix(slack): add explicit mention timeout fallback
target.body:
## What

Adds a Slack-visible fail-safe for explicit human room/channel mentions when the routed handler hits a Codex/app-server timeout before any assistant text, tool output, or other visible reply is delivered.

- Detects structured Codex `turn_completion_idle_timeout` metadata plus known timeout message shapes.
- Gates fallback delivery to explicit human Slack mentions only.
- Dedupes fallback posts for 24 hours by account, agent, Slack channel, and message timestamp.
- Routes fallback delivery through the normal Slack reply planner so top-level `replyToMode: "first"` mentions stay in the source-message thread and record thread participation.
- Preserves the original dispatch error path for diagnostics/status handling.
- Documents automatic retry as a follow-up because the timed-out handler may already have side-effecting work in flight.

Fixes #87783.
Refs #44925 and #58519.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: explicit Slack channel mentions that route successfully but hit a Codex/app-server handler timeout before visible output now post one deduped fallback message instead of staying silent.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS at PR head `6a4bfe5cf1ae3b7286b187ba1925d887f28d2b24`, rebased on upstream `main` base `51b69497a38f0defdfa3953aa791d620b0792650`, using the Slack extension production `dispatchPreparedSlackMessage` entrypoint through the repository Slack extension runner.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/clawdfamily/clawd-dev-team/worktrees/openclaw-slack-mention-failsafe-20260528

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  60 passed (60)
Start at  14:27:09
Duration  4.22s (transform 2.31s, setup 22ms, import 11ms, tests 4.08s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.15s
```

- Observed result after fix: the explicit-human-mention timeout path delivered exactly one outbound Slack fallback reply with text `I received this, but my handler timed out before producing a reply. Please retry in a fresh handler.` The same Slack message/account/agent was deduped on a second dispatch, while bot-authored explicit mentions and non-explicit room messages produced zero fallback replies. A top-level explicit mention with `replyToMode: "first"` delivered through the normal reply planner into the source-message thread and recorded thread participation for that thread.
- What was not tested: live Slack workspace delivery was not tested because this PR was not deployed or restarted. Automatic retry is intentionally out of scope for this slice.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` — passed, 60 tests after rebasing on `51b69497a38f0defdfa3953aa791d620b0792650`.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts docs/channels/slack.md CHANGELOG.md` — passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` — passed.

## Dependency / Freeze Note

No package.json, lockfile, shrinkwrap, credential, config, restart, deploy, or release change is included. While committing the earlier revision, the repo pre-commit hook unexpectedly invoked `pnpm exec oxfmt`, which bootstrapped local `node_modules` in the worktree and downloaded packages despite the active npm freeze. I moved the generated `node_modules` and `ui/node_modules` out of the worktree to `~/.Trash/openclaw-failsafe-generated-20260528T140734`; nothing from that install is committed. The final validation reused those already-created local dependencies via temporary symlinks and did not run another install/update.
target.author: jeremykraklist
